title: marsilea subangulata
intro: Marsilea subangulata, also known as the sub-angled waterclover or five-leaf fern, is a species of aquatic fern that belongs to the family Marsileaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve liver function due to its rich content of antioxidants and other medicinal properties. The health benefits of Marsilea subangulata include its potential to help manage conditions such as fever, skin diseases, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural diuretic. Marsilea subangulata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for its medicinal properties, and it can also be used in combination with other herbs in traditional herbal preparations.
